title: pyrus elaeagrifolia subsp. bulgarica
intro: Pyrus elaeagrifolia subsp. bulgarica, also known as the Bulgarian pear tree, is a subspecies of Pyrus elaeagrifolia, a deciduous tree native to Bulgaria. The bark and leaves of this plant have been traditionally used in folk medicine, with reported medicinal properties including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Pyrus elaeagrifolia subsp. bulgarica are said to include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system, although more research is needed to confirm these claims. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as an herbal tea, tincture, or infusion, often combined with other herbs, to utilize its medicinal properties.
